title: Scottish police hunt culprit after laser shone at search and rescue helicopter in 'reckless' act

sections.heading: What happened
sections.content: Police are investigating after a laser was allegedly shone at an HM Coastguard search and rescue helicopter in the Highlands at about 9.10pm on Friday 13 March. The aircraft was helping Glencoe Mountain Rescue Team in the Glen Etive area when the incident happened, and officers later appealed for witnesses to come forward.

sections.heading: Where officers focused
sections.content: According to Police Scotland, the light appeared to come from the eastern end of Glencoe in an area near the Kingshouse Hotel. Officers attended but did not trace whoever was responsible, and enquiries remain ongoing.

sections.heading: Police appeal
sections.content: Sergeant Brian Heriot said shining a laser at any aircraft was incredibly dangerous, reckless and a criminal act because crews must be able to operate safely and without distraction. Police asked anyone with information to call 101 immediately and quote incident number 2087 of Friday 13 March 2026, or contact Crimestoppers anonymously.

sourceReferences.citationText: scotsman-news – Scottish police hunt culprit after laser shone at search and rescue helicopter in ‘reckless’ act
